algae_description: Green coating visible across the rear wall and substrate — consistent with green algae colonization typical of an active nitrogen cycle around day 40. Appears widespread on the back glass and potentially on substrate.
substrate_condition: Partially visible beneath green algae growth; texture suggests gravel or coarse substrate but detail is limited by image angle and lighting.
concerns: Image is rotated ~90 degrees, limiting full assessment
Low image resolution and dark exposure reduce detail clarity
Significant green algae coating on rear glass and substrate — expected at day 40 of cycling but worth monitoring as shrimp are introduced
narrative: The tank is visible in this rotated, somewhat underexposed shot. The most striking feature is the lush green algae coating the rear wall and substrate — a healthy sign of a maturing nitrogen cycle at day 40. Two pieces of equipment are visible near the top (right when corrected): what appears to be a filter or powerhead with a teal/cyan intake tube, and a black device with a cord — likely a heater or secondary filter unit.
image_subject: full tank shot with equipment visible — filter intake (teal), heater or secondary device (black), rear glass with algae colonization

substrate_condition: Clean substrate visible, no notable debris accumulation observed.
concerns: Image is rotated 90 degrees and underexposed, limiting detailed inspection
Equipment visible (filter, heater probe, or sensor) but cannot fully assess condition due to lighting and angle
narrative: The tank is visible but the photo is rotated sideways and fairly dark, making a thorough scan challenging. Equipment is clearly present along the back wall — what appears to be a hang-on-back filter or pump and a probe or heater — and a cyan/teal-colored object (possibly a thermometer or water conditioner bottle) stands prominently in the mid-tank area. One small red-orange shrimp is faintly visible near the lower-mid frame, consistent with a Neocaridina cherry shrimp. Green growth on the back wall suggests plants or algae are present.

image_subject: equipment and filter media — top-down view of tank interior
substrate_condition: Brown granular substrate visible across the tank floor — appears clean with no visible debris accumulation
narrative: Top-down shot looking into the tank, catching a good slice of the interior: a mesh media bag packed with cylindrical bio-rings (likely ceramic or sintered glass) sits against what appears to be a back wall or divider, flanked by a pump or powerhead with its suction cup mount and intake assembly visible to the right. An LED light strip runs diagonally through the lower left, its warm white LEDs glowing just beneath the surface — a good sign the lighting is submerged and operational. A healthy cluster of bright green, small-leafed aquatic plant peeks in from the upper left corner, looking vivid and robust. The substrate below is uniform brown granular material, clean and undisturbed. No shrimp are visible in this frame, which is entirely expected given the tight equipment-focused framing.
